serving.natural_language_portion: One individual blueberry fruit cheese tart weighing approximately 90 g, as typically served at a Singaporean patisserie or café.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18147) and USDA Foundation Foods; adapted with blueberry topping adjustment using USDA FoodData Central blueberry data (FDC ID 171711)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Singapore-specific blueberry cheese tart entry found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (NDB 18147) as the primary structural analogue, with blueberry topping contribution estimated using USDA FoodData Central raw blueberry data (FDC ID 171711). Blueberry topping assumed at approximately 15% of total weight. Weighted blend applied to derive per-100g composite values. This is an estimated record; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — GI value from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283. Blueberry topping contribution estimated as negligible given low proportion.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for blueberry fruit cheese tart. GI of 46 is derived from cheesecake GI data reported in the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care), which is an approved peer-reviewed source. Blueberry topping (≈15% of serving) is a low-GI fruit and does not materially raise the composite GI. GL per serving calculated as (46 × 23.85 g available carbohydrate per 90 g serving) / 100 = 11.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100g) and saturated fat load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one small tart occasionally, as the moderate sodium content (~189 mg per 90 g serving) and saturated fat may contribute to blood pressure elevation with frequent consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate and sugar content (~16.8 g sugar/100g) with moderate GI of 46
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this tart sparingly; the moderate glycemic index (46) and high sugar content (~15.1 g sugar per 90 g serving) can raise postprandial blood glucose, and the energy-dense profile (~250 kcal per serving) may complicate glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~9.8 g/100g) and cholesterol (~88 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this tart as an occasional indulgence, as the saturated fat content (~8.8 g per 90 g serving) approaches a significant fraction of the recommended daily limit and may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Blueberry Fruit Cheese Tart is a chilled dessert pastry consisting of a buttery, baked shortcrust or pâte sucrée shell filled with a smooth, lightly sweetened cream cheese or mascarpone-based filling, topped with fresh blueberries and often finished with a blueberry compote or mirror glaze. It is a popular café and patisserie item in Singapore, commonly sold as an individual portion tart. The texture contrasts a crisp, crumbly pastry base with a dense, creamy, slightly tangy cheese filling and the soft, juicy burst of fresh blueberries. The flavour profile is sweet-tart with a rich dairy creaminess. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (~278 kcal/100g), moderate in protein (~5 g/100g), high in fat (~17.5 g/100g, predominantly saturated), and moderately high in sugar (~16.8 g/100g). The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low-medium range), making it a moderate glycaemic load food per serving. Clinically, it is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (moderate GI, high sugar), hypertension (moderate sodium), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and cholesterol). The blueberry topping contributes a small amount of dietary fibre, vitamin C, and antioxidant anthocyanins. This item is sold at Kazo Premium (Funan, Singapore) and similar premium patisseries across Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a blueberry cheese tart?
Is blueberry cheese tart suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of a cheese tart?
How much sugar in a blueberry fruit cheese tart?
Can I eat cheese tart if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact blueberry fruit cheese tart entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or ICMR-NIN databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake data (NDB 18147) with blueberry topping adjustment using USDA FoodData Central raw blueberry data (FDC ID 171711). GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheesecake) in the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008). Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (NDB 18147) as the primary structural analogue, with blueberry topping contribution (~15% of total weight) estimated using USDA FoodData Central raw blueberry data (FDC ID 171711). A weighted blend was applied to derive per-100g composite values. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source entry. GI of 46 is an analogous estimate based on cheesecake GI data from Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care, an approved peer-reviewed source. GL per serving = (46 × 23.85 g available carbohydrate per 90 g serving) / 100 = 11.2. The food_name has been normalised to remove the parenthetical '(Original)' qualifier, which is a menu variant descriptor rather than a canonical food name. The input food_group 'Dairy and eggs' has been preserved as it reflects the dominant ingredient profile (cream cheese, eggs, butter). Serving size of 90 g is estimated based on typical individual tart portion sizes at Singapore patisseries. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source with blueberry adjustment. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Hindi aliases 'ब्लूबेरी फ्रूट चीज़ टार्ट' and 'ब्लूबेरी टार्ट' use Devanagari script; Tamil aliases 'ப்ளூபெர்ரி பழ சீஸ் டார்ட்' and 'ப்ளூபெர்ரி டார்ட்' use Tamil script; Romanized alias 'Lan Mei Shui Guo Zhi Shi Ta' is also present.
